usage: get_physical_info [-help] [-highlight] [-shapes {RING STRIPE FOLLOWPIN IOWIRE COREWIRE BLOCKWIRE PADRING BLOCKRING FILLWIRE FILLWIREOPC DRCFILL None}] [-subclass {subclass_name_list}] [-type {connection routing_path}] {-object <string> | -selected }

options.name: -object
options.description: Specifies the object from which to get the physical information. For bump, it's required to input the whole bump name and for IO instance, it can input inst_name, inst_name:pin_name or inst_name:pin_name:port_number.

options.name: -selected
options.description: Get the physical information for the selected object

options.name: -shapes
options.description: {RING STRIPE FOLLOWPIN IOWIRE COREWIRE BLOCKWIRE PADRING BLOCKRING FILLWIRE FILLWIREOPC DRCFILL None} Specifies the shapes of the wires and vias to be checked. The default value is IOWIRE.

options.name: -subclass
options.description: Specifies the subclass name of the wires and vias to be checked

options.name: -type
options.description: Specifies the type of physical information to return. The default value is connection.
